name: Circular Magnetic + Gravity High
description: Coincident circular magnetic and gravity highs suggest dense, magnetic body — classic mafic intrusion signature.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Anomaly pattern is consistent with a {{entity}}. Coincident magnetic and gravity highs with circular geometry and sharp gradients support this interpretation.
additionalRecommendations: Consider analytic signal to constrain depth estimate
Bouguer correction required to isolate residual gravity

name: Linear Elongated Magnetic High
description: Linear elongated magnetic high consistent with dyke or BIF horizon.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Linear elongated magnetic high is consistent with a {{entity}} or linear magnetic unit. Orientation analysis recommended to constrain emplacement direction.
additionalRecommendations: Run RTP to remove inclination bias
Extract lineament azimuth for structural analysis

name: Linear Magnetic Low — Shear Zone
description: Linear magnetic low in otherwise magnetic terrane suggests magnetite destruction along a shear zone.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Linear magnetic low is consistent with a {{entity}} — magnetite destruction along ductile deformation zone is a common cause of this pattern.
additionalRecommendations: Coincident resistivity survey recommended to detect graphitic/sulphide shear fabric
Check orientation against known structural trends

name: Demagnetisation Halo
description: Broad irregular magnetic low in mineralised terrane suggests alteration halo with magnetite destruction.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Irregular magnetic low is consistent with an {{entity}} — hydrothermal alteration commonly destroys magnetite, producing this pattern.
additionalRecommendations: Analytic signal may resolve deeper magnetic sources beneath demagnetised zone

name: Circular Magnetic Low — Felsic Intrusion
description: Circular magnetic low with coincident gravity low suggests felsic (granitic) intrusion.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Coincident circular magnetic and gravity lows are consistent with a {{entity}} — low-density, low-susceptibility granitoid body.
additionalRecommendations: Euler deconvolution may estimate depth to source
Gravity modelling recommended for volume estimate

name: RTP Instability Risk
description: Airborne magnetic data acquired near magnetic equator — RTP filter may be unstable.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Processing assumption: if survey latitude is within ±15° of magnetic equator, RTP filter is potentially unstable. Analytic signal (inclination-independent) is recommended as primary derivative.
additionalRecommendations: Use analytic signal instead of RTP for low-latitude surveys

name: Cultural Noise Risk
description: Discrete, non-geological short-wavelength anomalies may indicate cultural interference.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Uncertainty warning: cultural noise (pipelines, fences, vehicles) may be contributing to short-wavelength magnetic anomalies. Interpretation confidence is reduced until cultural contamination is assessed.
additionalRecommendations: Apply microlevelling if not already done
Cross-reference anomaly locations with infrastructure maps

name: Contact Aureole Magnetic High Ring
description: Annular or asymmetric magnetic high surrounding a central magnetic low — may indicate metamorphic aureole around felsic body.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Magnetic pattern is consistent with a {{entity}} — magnetite generation during contact metamorphism produces high adjacent to the intrusion.
additionalRecommendations: Map gradient zones to define intrusion boundary
Field validation of magnetite content at gradient zone
